titlepage.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections Cornell University Library

defitem.label: Contact Information:

did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.physdesc: Correspondence, diaries, photographs, squeezes, sketches, and ephemera.
did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library
did.abstract: Correspondence, travel diaries, official documents, and photographs. Letters from Andrews to his family from Italy, Greece, Crete, and Egypt describing the progress of his archaeological work, personal matters, travels, and activities.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, travel diaries, official documents, and photographs. Letters from Andrews to his family from Italy, Greece, Crete, and Egypt describing the progress of his archaeological work, personal matters, travels, and activities; an account of the Olympic Games, material pertaining to the Cornell Class of 1895, a gift of Tiffany Favrile glass to the university, the University chimes, and athletics. Correspondents include Alfred Emerson, Willard Straight, Hendrik Willem van Loon, and Bejamin Ide Wheeler.

Also, correspondence, notes, archaeological squeezes, sketches, and photographs relating to Andrews' years in Athens as a student at the American School of Classical Studies. Includes descriptions of duels at the University of Heidelberg, and trips in Switzerland, Sicily, and the Middle East with his wife Helen Putnam Andrews. Also, letters and sketches from van Loon, and notes, programs, tickets, and a newspaper relating to the 1896 Olympic Games in Athens.

controlaccess.head: Names:
controlaccess.corpname: American School of Classical Studies (Athens, Greece)

scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Eugene Plumb Andrews papers, #14-6-912. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
